channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fani Willis disqualification may doom Trump’s Georgia case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis’s (D) disqualification from Georgia's election interference case against President-elect Trump and his allies could mark the beginning of the end of the prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida requires teaching Black history. Some don't trust schools to do it justice
item.description: Thirty years after Florida required schools to teach African American history, how the subject is taught remains inconsistent across Florida classrooms, a review by The Associated Press has found.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adams says Dems 'missed the memo' on migrant crisis and it hurt the party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams sat down with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino for an exclusive year-end interview, looking back at what was undoubtedly a very eventful 2024 and ahead to what’s in store for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Government funding difficulties create gloom for federal workers before Christmas
item.description: Johnny Zuagar says he’s tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House pressed on Biden refusing to speak publicly ahead of shutdown
item.description: The White House refused to discuss President Biden’s lack of public appearances amid the ongoing spending bill crisis as a government shutdown looms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US drops $10M terrorism bounty offered for capture of Syrian rebel leader who ousted Assad
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The Biden administration said Friday it has decided not to pursue a $10 million reward it had offered for the capture of a Syrian rebel leader whose forces led the ouster of President Bashar Assad earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada election leaders find nearly 200 cases of double votes from November
item.description: The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office is investigating 180 double votes from the 2024 general election, a report released Friday indicated.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House pressed on Biden's absence during funding talks
item.description: White House Press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre was pressed Friday about why President Biden has stayed out of the public eye when it comes to government funding talks that are gripping Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump adds Europe to the list of US trade partners he's threatening with tariffs
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday added the 27 countries that make up the European Union to the list of trade partners he's threatening with tariffs — unless the group takes steps to import more U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost about 2.1 million troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say lines of communication open with GOP on funding, but no bill presented
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) told his party that lines of communication have reopened between his office and Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) to avert a government shutdown this weekend, members said.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden heading to Vatican City next month to meet with Pope Francis, Meloni in final overseas trip
item.description: The White House announced that President Joe Biden will head to Italy early next month for a public meeting with Pope Francis in Vatican City. During the trip he will also hold meetings with Italy's Prime Minister and President.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Senate Dems pour cold water on latest GOP spending bill plans: 'Ready to stay' through Christmas
item.description: Top Democrats urged Republicans to return to the original stopgap spending bill in order to avoid a partial government shutdown at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: Vance flexes his influence during spending fight
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance is flexing his influence on Capitol Hill, underscoring his role as a critical and often behind-the-scenes player in President-elect Trump’s orbit while other prominent figures steal the headlines.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump border czar: 100K beds, more ICE agents needed for deportations
item.description: “This will be an expense — this will be an expensive operation. But in the long run, it should be a — it would be a huge tax savings on the American people,” Homan said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Musk demands Republicans sink spending bill
item.description: Musk whipped up outrage toward the bill and cheered on Republican lawmakers who announced their opposition.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Spending bill contains relief for border farmers affected by lack of water
item.description: A spending bill that Congress is debating to continue funding the federal government past Friday also contains funding relief for farmers on the South Texas border who have been affected by a lack of water payments from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: How much power would RFK Jr. have at HHS? A former health secretary weighs in
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is making the case with senators that he should lead Health and Human Services. Kathleen Sebelius, who had the job under Obama, explains the power and limits of the role.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gorenstein, MPR News

item.title: Ryan Routh, alleged would-be Trump assassin, faces new felony charge
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody obtained an arrest warrant for Ryan Routh on an unrelated charge of attempted felony murder involving a six-year-old in a car accident.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: After losing VP bid, Walz searches for role in pushing back against Trump
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz saw his national profile skyrocket when he became Vice President Harris’ running mate. Now, he’s back home figuring out when he will push back or work with Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams expected in court on criminal charges: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Federal Reserve cuts its key rate by a quarter-point but envisions fewer reductions next year
item.description: The Federal Reserve cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point — its third cut this year — but also signaled that it expects to reduce rates more slowly next year than it previously envisioned, largely because of still-elevated inflation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: The Senate has passed a defense bill that authorize significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members and boost overall military spending to $895 billion. It also strips coverage of transgender medical treatments for children of military members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if it's not sold
item.description: The justices said Wednesday they will hear arguments Jan. 10 about whether the law impermissibly restricts speech in violation of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP lawmaker files bill banning political flags in public buildings
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
